challenges.title: Emergency Situation
challenges.question: Your bike needs urgent repairs costing ₹500. You have ₹300 saved. What do you do?

feedback.correct: Smart choice! Using savings and earning more shows financial responsibility!
feedback.wrong: Remember: Use your savings first and earn more rather than borrowing unnecessarily.

challenges.title: Investment Opportunity
challenges.question: A friend offers 50% return on ₹1000 investment in 1 month. What's your choice?

feedback.correct: Excellent! High returns in short time are usually too good to be true!
feedback.wrong: Be cautious! Unrealistic returns often indicate scams or high risk.

challenges.title: Shopping Temptation
challenges.question: You see a ₹2000 gadget you want, but you're saving for college fees. Do you buy it?

feedback.correct: Perfect! Prioritizing important goals over wants is smart saving!
feedback.wrong: Remember: Important goals like education should come before wants.

challenges.title: Bonus Dilemma
challenges.question: You receive ₹1000 bonus. Should you save it all or spend some?

feedback.correct: Great balance! Saving most while enjoying a small reward is wise!
feedback.wrong: A good rule: Save most of unexpected money, but allow a small reward for motivation.

challenges.title: Peer Pressure
challenges.question: Friends are planning an expensive trip. You can't afford it but don't want to miss out. What do you do?

feedback.correct: Smart! Finding affordable alternatives shows financial maturity!
feedback.wrong: Don't let peer pressure lead to poor financial decisions. Suggest affordable options!

result.winMessage: You made {{score}} smart saving decisions out of {{total}} challenges!
result.smartChoicesText: You chose to save for emergencies, avoid risky investments, prioritize important goals, and resist peer pressure spending.
result.wisdomText: These habits will help you build wealth and achieve your long-term financial goals!
result.loseMessage: You made {{score}} smart saving decisions out of {{total}} challenges.
result.loseAdvice: Remember, smart saving means prioritizing important goals, avoiding risky investments, and making thoughtful financial decisions.
